assets.name: lecture_03_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the clearest and most complete frame. By this point Susskind has moved far enough left that the boxed derivative condition on the right is fully visible, while the left-board coordinate-expansion formula and the central geometric sketch are still legible. Earlier candidates are more heavily occluded by his body and writing arm.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: that the derivatives of a vector are equal to zero,

assets.name: lecture_03_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 gives the clearest and most complete board state. Susskind has moved far enough left that the three central conditions on the metric in Gaussian normal coordinates are legible with minimal occlusion, while the auxiliary coordinate-expansion note on the right remains visible. Earlier candidates obstruct more of the equations.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: But there's something special about the metric tensor.
